heading: Approval of organization designation authorization unit members
label: 44741 Approval of organization designation authorization unit members

children.label: A
children.text: an employee, a contractor, or a consultant of the ODA holder; or

children.label: B
children.text: the employee of a supplier of the ODA holder; and
may not become a member of such unit unless approved by the Administrator pursuant to this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall maintain an efficient process for the review and approval of an individual to become an ODA unit member under this section.

children.heading: .—Process
children.label: 2 .—Process
children.text: An ODA holder described in subsection (a) may submit to the Administrator an application for an individual to be approved to become an ODA unit member under this section. The application shall be submitted in such form and manner as the Administrator determines appropriate. The Administrator shall require an ODA holder to submit with such an application information sufficient to demonstrate an individual’s qualifications under subsection (c).

children.heading: .—Timeline
children.label: 3 .—Timeline
children.text: The Administrator shall approve or reject an individual that is selected by an ODA holder to become an ODA unit member under this section not later than 30 days after the receipt of an application by an ODA holder.

children.heading: .—Documentation of approval
children.label: 4 .—Documentation of approval
children.text: Upon approval of an individual to become an ODA unit member under this section, the Administrator shall provide such individual a letter confirming that such individual has been approved by the Administrator under this section to be an ODA unit member.

children.heading: .—Reapplication
children.label: 5 .—Reapplication
children.text: An ODA holder may submit an application under this subsection for an individual to become an ODA unit member under this section regardless of whether an application for such individual was previously rejected by the Administrator.

children.label: A
children.text: is technically proficient and qualified to perform the authorized functions sought;

children.label: B
children.text: has no recent record of serious enforcement action, as determined by the Administrator, taken by the Administrator with respect to any certificate, approval, or authorization held by such individual;

children.label: C
children.text: is of good moral character (as such qualification is applied to an applicant for an airline transport pilot certificate issued under section 44703);

children.label: D
children.text: possesses the knowledge of applicable design or production requirements in this chapter and in title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, necessary for performance of the authorized functions sought;

children.label: E
children.text: possesses a high degree of knowledge of applicable design or production principles, system safety principles, or safety risk management processes appropriate for the authorized functions sought; and

children.label: F
children.text: meets such testing, examination, training, or other qualification standards as the Administrator determines are necessary to ensure the individual is competent and capable of performing the authorized functions sought.

children.heading: .—Previously rejected application
children.label: 2 .—Previously rejected application
children.text: In reviewing an application for an individual to become an ODA unit member under this section, if an application for such individual was previously rejected, the Administrator shall ensure that the reasons for the prior rejection have been resolved or mitigated to the Administrator’s satisfaction before making a determination on the individual’s reapplication.

children.heading: .—Rescission of Approval
children.label: d .—Rescission of Approval
children.text: The Administrator may rescind an approval of an individual as an ODA unit member granted pursuant to this section at any time and for any reason the Administrator considers appropriate. The Administrator shall develop procedures to provide for notice and opportunity to appeal rescission decisions made by the Administrator. Such decisions by the Administrator are not subject to judicial review.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to the requirements of this subsection, the Administrator may authorize an ODA holder to conditionally designate an individual to perform the functions of an ODA unit member for a period of not more than 30 days (beginning on the date an application for such individual is submitted under subsection (b)(2)).

children.label: A
children.text: the ODA holder has instituted, to the Administrator’s satisfaction, systems and processes to ensure the integrity and reliability of determinations by conditionally-designated ODA unit members; and

children.label: B
children.text: the ODA holder has instituted a safety management system in accordance with regulations issued by the Administrator under section 102 of the Aircraft Certification, Safety, and Accountability Act.

children.heading: .—Final determination
children.label: 3 .—Final determination
children.text: The Administrator shall approve or reject the application for an individual designated under paragraph (1) in accordance with the timeline and procedures described in subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Rejection and review
children.label: 4 .—Rejection and review
children.text: If the Administrator rejects the application submitted under subsection (b)(2) for an individual conditionally designated under paragraph (1), the Administrator shall review and approve or disapprove any decision pursuant to any authorized function performed by such individual during the period such individual served as a conditional designee.

children.heading: .—Prohibitions
children.label: 5 .—Prohibitions
children.text: Notwithstanding the requirements of paragraph (2), the Administrator may prohibit an ODA holder from making conditional designations of individuals as ODA unit members under this subsection at any time for any reason the Administrator considers appropriate. The Administrator may prohibit any conditionally designated individual from performing an authorized function at any time for any reason the Administrator considers appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: any data, applications, records, or manuals required by the ODA holder’s approved procedures manual, as determined by the Administrator;

children.label: B
children.text: the names, responsibilities, qualifications, and example signature of each member of the ODA unit who performs an authorized function pursuant to a delegation by the Administrator under section 44702(d);

children.label: C
children.text: training records for ODA unit members and ODA administrators; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other data, applications, records, or manuals determined appropriate by the Administrator.

children.label: A
children.text: the Administration’s performance in completing reviews of individuals and approving or denying such individuals within the timeline required under subsection (b)(3);

children.label: B
children.text: for any individual rejected by the Administrator under subsection (b) during the preceding 6-month period, the reasoning or basis for such rejection; and

children.label: C
children.text: any resource, staffing, or other challenges within the Administration associated with implementation of this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the issuance of minimum qualifications under subsection (c), the Administrator shall initiate a review of the qualifications of each individual who on the date on which such minimum qualifications are issued is an ODA unit member of a holder of a type certificate for a transport airplane to ensure such individual meets the minimum qualifications issued by the Administrator under subsection (c).

children.label: A
children.text: shall determine whether the lack of qualification may be remedied and, if so, provide such individual with an action plan or schedule for such individual to meet such qualifications; or

children.label: B
children.text: may, if the Administrator determines the lack of qualification may not be remedied, take appropriate action, including prohibiting such individual from performing an authorized function.

children.heading: .—Deadline
children.label: 3 .—Deadline
children.text: The Administrator shall complete the review required under paragraph (1) not later than 18 months after the date on which such review was initiated.

children.heading: .—Savings clause
children.label: 4 .—Savings clause
children.text: An individual approved to become an ODA unit member of a holder of a type certificate for a transport airplane under subsection (a) shall not be subject to the review under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Prohibition
children.label: h .—Prohibition
children.text: The Administrator may not authorize an organization or ODA holder to approve an individual selected by an ODA holder to become an ODA unit member under this section.

children.heading: .—General applicability
children.label: 1 .—General applicability
children.text: The definitions contained in section 44736(c) shall apply to this section.

children.heading: .—Transport airplane
children.label: 2 .—Transport airplane
children.text: The term “transport airplane” means a transport category airplane designed for operation by an air carrier or foreign air carrier type-certificated with a passenger seating capacity of 30 or more or an all-cargo or combi derivative of such an airplane.

children.heading: .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.label: j .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $3,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2028.
